task.title: Replacing /etc/resolv.conf with a symbolic link to manually configure DNS settings

li.p: The NetworkManager rc-manager configuration option is not set to file. To verify, use the NetworkManager --print-config command.

context.p: By default, NetworkManager manages DNS settings in the /etc/resolv.conf file, and you can configure the order of DNS servers. Alternatively, you can disable DNS processing in NetworkManager if you prefer to manually configure DNS settings in /etc/resolv.conf. For example, NetworkManager does not automatically update the DNS configuration if /etc/resolv.conf is a symbolic link.

step.cmd: Create a file, such as /etc/resolv.conf.manually-configured, and add the DNS configuration for your environment to it. Use the same parameters and syntax as in the original /etc/resolv.conf.
Create a symbolic link named /etc/resolv.conf that refers to /etc/resolv.conf.manually-configured:

p.xref: Configuring the order of DNS servers using NetworkManager
